persona.description: male, 25, desk job, has tried and quit every diet
persona.goal: stopped restarting — tracked one daily score instead of five apps

segments.text: Then he tracked one score instead of five apps.

visual.motion: Health Score screen, score ticking up

segments.text: It just shows you what to fix.

visual.motion: coach chat mockup, one nudge message

assets.before_prompt: unedited amateur photo taken on a phone, slightly grainy, harsh overhead fluorescent lighting, realistic ordinary body, imperfect skin with natural texture, candid slightly awkward posture, no retouching, no studio lighting, muted colors, looks like a real photo posted on reddit. A tired 25-year-old man in a wrinkled t-shirt taking a mirror selfie in a cluttered bedroom at night, soft around the middle, flat expression.
assets.after_prompt: Keep the EXACT same person, face, and identity as the reference. Same location and framing, same amateur phone-photo quality. Believable result of 90 days of consistent training — NOT a fitness model; still has natural soft spots, just visibly leaner and healthier. Same mirror selfie, room slightly tidier, shirt fits better, shoulders back, small genuine smile.

audio.vibe: quiet lo-fi build that opens up at the day-90 crossfade
audio.source: licensed/trending track chosen at assembly, muxed with -shortest

caption:
He didn't need another diet. He needed to see the whole picture in one place. 📊

90 days of tracking sleep, food, water & steps as one score. No perfect weeks — just never two bad days in a row. Save this if you keep restarting. 🔖

Vibe Health turns it all into one daily score that shows you what to fix → link in bio.
youtube_title: 90 Days of Tracking One Score Changed Everything
notes: Reveal beat (after segment) carries the audio drop; keep reveal text minimal.
